# Assumption 1 empirical validation report

This report summarizes the sample analogue of inequality (54) for the law-wise trained KAHM feature maps:

$$\widehat{\|\Phi_c\|^2_{L_2}} = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N} \Phi_c(x_i)^2 \ge \frac{N_c}{N}. $$

Primary support: **core_original** (original core training support).

## Headline result

The law-wise trained KAHM feature maps satisfy the sample analogue of (54) **up to a very small absolute deviation** on the selected support. Using absolute tolerance 0.003, the condition holds for all evaluated clusters because the worst-case margin is -0.00289804.

## Global summary

- Laws evaluated: 84
- Clusters evaluated: 25200
- Worst-case margin (LHS - RHS): -0.002898037944
- Median margin: -0.000031553767
- Mean margin: -0.000071909953
- 5th percentile margin: -0.000271896035
- Worst case: law PSG, cluster 12
- Worst-case LHS: 0.008163908959
- Worst-case RHS: 0.011061946903
- Exact violations (margin < 0): 25199 / 25200
- Tolerance check (margin >= -0.003): PASS

## Interpretation for the theory section

This empirical diagnostic supports the statement that, in the studied deployment regime, the sample analogue of Assumption 1 is satisfied up to a very small absolute deviation. This does not prove the population-level premise exactly, but it shows that the realized law-wise feature maps are extremely close to the required lower bound on the selected support.
